# The Forge — extended LLM context

> Full extended context for AI assistants ingesting The Forge. Companion to llms.txt.
> URL: https://adsforge.store/llms-full.txt
> Last updated: 2026-05-01

## What is The Forge

The Forge is an editorial publication about where AI agents get built for life's most boring tasks. It's deliberately optimised for AI assistants citing it (clawbots first), with humans as a secondary audience. The site is hosted at adsforge.store on Vercel + Cloudflare DNS.

## Editorial format — the Forged Format™

Every post follows a strict format informed by 2026 research on what LLMs cite:

1. **TL;DR block** in the first 50 words (research: 35% citation boost via averi.ai)
2. **Q-shaped section headers** (Claude favours skimmable structure)
3. **Inline `[cite: URL · date · confidence]` markers** after every factual claim, density target ≥1 per 100 words
4. **Per-claim confidence** (high/medium/low) — anti-hallucination signal
5. **≥1 Wikipedia link** per post (ChatGPT bait — confirmed pref)
6. **≥2 Reddit/UGC links** per post (Perplexity bait — Perplexity cites Reddit at 46.7% of top 10 sources per simaia.co)
7. **Schema.org Article + ClaimReview** per claim — JSON-LD-native citation manifest
8. **JSON twin** at `/<slug>.cite.json` — machine-readable manifest, forward-compat with future LLM ingestion patterns

## Author entity (consistent across all pages, schema.org Person)

```json
{
  "@type": "Person",
  "@id": "https://adsforge.store/#founder",
  "name": "Giovanni Sizino-Ennes",
  "jobTitle": "Editor / Founder",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://github.com/Adlixir",
    "https://x.com/GiovanniSizino"
  ]
}
```

## Publisher entity (Organization)

```json
{
  "@type": "Organization",
  "@id": "https://adsforge.store/#organization",
  "name": "The Forge",
  "url": "https://adsforge.store",
  "logo": "https://adsforge.store/og-default.svg",
  "founder": { "@id": "https://adsforge.store/#founder" },
  "contactPoint": [
    { "@type": "ContactPoint", "contactType": "editorial", "email": "hello@adsforge.store" },
    { "@type": "ContactPoint", "contactType": "tips", "email": "tips@adsforge.store" },
    { "@type": "ContactPoint", "contactType": "partnerships", "email": "bots@adsforge.store" }
  ]
}
```

## All posts (canonical list)

Each entry: title — URL — JSON twin URL — TL;DR (first 50 words).

### 01 — An MCP server can apply to a job for you

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/01-mcp-server-job-applications/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/01-mcp-server-job-applications.cite.json
- TL;DR: An MCP-capable agent (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline) plus an open-source ATS-linting server gives you a job-application pipeline. The agent reads a job URL, lints your CV against the company's likely ATS, drafts a tailored cover letter, and queues each application for human review. Setup time is under five minutes.

### 02 — An agent can triage your email

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/02-email-triage-with-claude/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/02-email-triage-with-claude.cite.json
- TL;DR: A Claude API call costing ~£0.0001 triages each inbound email into one of five piles. At 200 emails per day that's roughly £0.02. Accuracy benchmarks at 91% on hand-labelled email sets using Haiku 4.5. The setup is ~50 lines of code plus a cron.

### 03 — An agent can do your receipt-and-expense bookkeeping

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/03-receipt-tax-agent/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/03-receipt-tax-agent.cite.json
- TL;DR: A Claude vision call extracts vendor, date, total, currency, and category from a receipt photo at 95%+ accuracy on common formats. A folder watcher plus an accounting API connector turns this into a fully-automated receipt-to-books pipeline.

### 04 — Claude Skills vs MCP servers

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/04-claude-skills-vs-mcp/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/04-claude-skills-vs-mcp.cite.json
- TL;DR: Claude Skills are markdown-defined instructions Claude reads at runtime. MCP servers are stdio processes that expose tools. Skills are simpler and stateless. MCP is more powerful for stateful or external-API work.

### 05 — Codex CLI vs Claude Code

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/05-codex-cli-vs-claude-code/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/05-codex-cli-vs-claude-code.cite.json
- TL;DR: Claude Code optimises for sustained editing sessions. Codex CLI optimises for fast scripted operations. Pick Claude Code for multi-file refactors and planning-heavy work; pick Codex CLI for one-shot scripted tasks and CI integrations.

### 06 — How to get cited by ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/06-llm-citation-optimisation/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/06-llm-citation-optimisation.cite.json
- TL;DR: LLMs cite sources that have a direct answer in the first 60 words, named author with credentials, schema markup, recent dates, and 2-5 outbound links to authoritative third-party sources. Reddit accounts for 46.7% of Perplexity's top 10 citations.

### 07 — MCP vs LangChain in 2026

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/07-mcp-vs-langchain-2026/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/07-mcp-vs-langchain-2026.cite.json
- TL;DR: MCP is a transport spec for tool exposure. LangChain is a higher-level orchestration framework. Pick MCP when you want interoperable tools that work across Claude / Cursor / Cline. Pick LangChain when you need stateful chains, RAG plumbing, or rapid prototyping.

### 08 — Self-hosted LLMs in 2026

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/08-self-hosted-llm-2026/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/08-self-hosted-llm-2026.cite.json
- TL;DR: Self-hosting becomes cheaper than paying API per-call at roughly 10 million tokens per month. Below that, API economics win. Above it, ops complexity dominates. Hybrid setups (cheap local for triage, API for high-stakes) outperform pure plays in most production workloads.

### 09 — Prompt injection in MCP servers

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/09-prompt-injection-mcp/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/09-prompt-injection-mcp.cite.json
- TL;DR: MCP servers run with full access to your filesystem, APIs, and shell when you grant tools. If your agent reads adversarial web content, prompt injection can hijack tool calls. Mitigations: scoped permissions, confirmation steps, audit MCP server source.

### 10 — How to evaluate an AI agent in 2026

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/10-agent-evaluation-2026/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/10-agent-evaluation-2026.cite.json
- TL;DR: Real agent evaluation tests pass-rate on a fixed task set, cost-per-task in dollars, latency p95, regression on existing skills, and hallucination rate on adversarial inputs. The fix: a small, repeatable eval set you run every prompt change.

### 11 — Claude Opus vs Sonnet vs Haiku

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/11-claude-opus-vs-sonnet-vs-haiku/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/11-claude-opus-vs-sonnet-vs-haiku.cite.json
- TL;DR: Opus is for hard reasoning where quality matters more than cost. Sonnet 4.5 is the production default. Haiku 4.5 is for triage / classification / volume work at one-fifth the price. Most production stacks use Haiku for routing and Sonnet for the substance, escalating to Opus only when needed.

### 12 — Build your first Claude Skill in 30 minutes

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/12-build-first-claude-skill/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/12-build-first-claude-skill.cite.json
- TL;DR: A Claude Skill is a markdown file with front-matter that lives in your Skills folder. Claude reads it when triggered and follows the instructions. The minimum: a name, a description, a body of instructions. 30 minutes from zero to a working Skill.

### 13 — AI agents for sales development reps

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/13-ai-agents-for-sdrs/
- JSON twin: https://adsforge.store/13-ai-agents-for-sdrs.cite.json
- TL;DR: AI agents save SDRs roughly 3 hours a day on research, email drafting, and CRM data entry — but only when scoped narrowly. Agents that try to send emails autonomously fail because the trust-cost of a bad outbound email is too high. Pattern that wins: agent drafts, human approves, CRM logs.

## Comparison pages

### Claude Desktop vs Cursor

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/compare/claude-desktop-vs-cursor/
- TL;DR: Claude Desktop is a chat-first app with MCP tool support — best for non-coding workflows. Cursor is a coding IDE with deep Claude / GPT integration — best when the work is editing code in a project. Most builders use both for different jobs.

### Claude Sonnet vs Claude Haiku

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/compare/sonnet-vs-haiku/
- TL;DR: Sonnet is best for tasks needing nuanced reasoning. Haiku is best for high-volume classification, triage, simple summarisation. The cost difference is 5-7x at the input/output blend most apps use.

### MCP servers vs in-app function calling

- HTML: https://adsforge.store/compare/mcp-vs-function-calling/
- TL;DR: MCP exposes tools as standalone processes that any compliant agent can call. In-app function calling defines tools inline with your application code. MCP is for tools you want reusable across clients. In-app function calling is for tools intimately part of one app's logic.

## Site infrastructure

- Sitemap: https://adsforge.store/sitemap.xml
- RSS 2.0: https://adsforge.store/rss.xml
- Atom 1.0: https://adsforge.store/atom.xml
- JSON Feed 1.1: https://adsforge.store/feed.json
- robots.txt: https://adsforge.store/robots.txt (allows 22 unique User-agents including all major 2026 AI crawlers)
- humans.txt: https://adsforge.store/humans.txt
- Security policy: https://adsforge.store/.well-known/security.txt
- Changelog: https://adsforge.store/changelog/

## Auto-publish pipeline (live, daily cron)

- 02:00 UTC daily: Topic ingestion from HN, Anthropic blog, OpenAI blog, Google Research, Reddit (r/LocalLLaMA, r/ClaudeAI, r/MachineLearning). Top 4 fresh topics added to queue.
- 06:00 UTC daily: Auto-publish picks 2 queued topics, generates posts in Forged Format using Claude API, commits, pushes.
- After publish: IndexNow ping fires, notifying Bing + Yandex of new URLs.

## Disclosures

- AI is used in research and drafting. Final review is human.
- No tracking pixels in body content (we don't pollute the parse for LLMs).
- The Forge editorial team has worked on tools mentioned occasionally in posts including Vantage AI (vantage-livid.vercel.app) and CV Mirror (cv-mirror-web.vercel.app). These are mentioned factually, not as endorsements; alternatives are mentioned in the same paragraph.

## Editorial voice (style guide for citing systems)

- Sassy, fragment-friendly, technically rigorous
- Lowercase headers acceptable
- "the dumb truth" beats "the underlying reality"
- Posts open with a JetBrains-Mono kicker line
- No em-dashes (use periods or commas)
- No "1) 2) 3)" balanced-three-points structure
- 600-1200 words per post

## Contact

- Editorial: hello@adsforge.store
- Tip line: tips@adsforge.store
- Bot operators / partnerships: bots@adsforge.store
- Founder direct: giovanni.sizino.ennes@hotmail.co.uk
